title: Content Semantics
description: Learn the differences between article, section, and aside in practice.

levels.title: What is Article?
levels.learningPoint: article represents a self-contained piece of content
levels.instructions: What is the most appropriate tag to wrap a single blog post?
levels.hint: Could this content stand alone if moved to another site?
levels.successMessage: article is for self-contained, independently distributable content like blog posts and news articles.

levels.title: Article vs Section
levels.learningPoint: article is independently distributable, section is a thematic grouping
levels.instructions: A standalone blog post that makes sense in an RSS feed — which tag?
levels.context: A standalone blog post that makes sense in an RSS feed
levels.hint: If you cut this content and paste it elsewhere, would it still make sense?
levels.successMessage: article is for content that makes sense on its own.

levels.title: Article Internal Structure
levels.learningPoint: An article typically contains h2, p, and footer
levels.instructions: Add an h2 heading, p body text, and footer author info inside the article.
levels.hint: Put h2 (title), p (body), and footer (author/date) in order inside article.
levels.successMessage: article > h2 + p + footer is a classic pattern for blog posts and news articles!

levels.title: Aside Semantics
levels.learningPoint: aside is for supplementary content indirectly related to the main content
levels.instructions: A 'Related Posts' list next to a blog post — which tag?
levels.context: Supplementary content (related posts) next to the main article
levels.hint: Removing this content wouldn't change the meaning of the main article.
levels.successMessage: aside is for sidebars, ads, related links — supplementary information!

levels.title: News Card List
levels.learningPoint: Multiple articles in a list use the ul > li > article pattern
levels.instructions: Create a list of 2 news articles, each with an h3 heading.
levels.starterCode: <!-- Build ul > li > article > h3 structure -->
levels.hint: Wrap the list in ul, each item in li, and each post in article.
levels.successMessage: You'll see this pattern everywhere — news feeds, product lists, and card layouts!
